source_line_numbering_note: line numbers refer to the 2026-03-19 working article text that includes the implementation-status note preface; they do not reuse the older gap-analysis line numbering

blockers.title: Declared article-equivalent frontend/compiler ingress remains open
blockers.current_gap_summary: the article claims arbitrary C-code ingress into an in-transformer execution path, while the public repo closes only a bounded Rust-only frontend route
blockers.current_public_truth: the repo already has committed Rust source canon, Rust-to-Wasm profile completeness, bounded ABI closure, and a green Rust-only article closeout audit, but that is still narrower than the article's frontend rhetoric
closure_requirements: declare one article frontend/compiler envelope instead of reusing the Rust-only closeout envelope by implication
expand the committed source corpus and compile matrix over that envelope
close the Hungarian and Sudoku demo sources through that declared envelope

article_line_refs.claim_paraphrase: the article claims arbitrary C code can be turned into tokens and executed by the model
the article frames the interpreter as a WebAssembly target that languages such as C and C++ can compile to

blockers.title: Declared article interpreter breadth remains open
blockers.current_gap_summary: the article frames arbitrary programs and a WebAssembly interpreter inside transformer weights, while the public repo still carries bounded named Wasm profiles and a suppressed frozen core-Wasm closure gate
blockers.current_public_truth: the repo has a real bounded Wasm lane, a declared frozen core-Wasm window, and a bounded Turing-complete substrate statement under `TCM.v1`, but not a public generic interpreter-in-weights breadth claim
closure_requirements: declare one article interpreter breadth envelope rather than relying on bounded Rust-only article fixtures
build a broad program-family suite over the declared article interpreter envelope
keep refusal and unsupported-program taxonomy machine-legible when breadth remains incomplete

article_line_refs.claim_paraphrase: the article claims arbitrary programs can execute inside a transformer
the article claims the transformer executes the emitted program itself step by step within the same transformer
the article claims a WebAssembly interpreter inside the transformer weights

blockers.title: Canonical owned Transformer-backed article route remains open
blockers.current_gap_summary: the article-equivalent path still lacks one canonical owned Transformer stack and one non-fixture article model artifact that carries the public route end to end
blockers.current_public_truth: the repo now has real tensor, array, nn, runtime, and `psionic-transformer` substrate overlap, but the public article-closeout proof surface is still narrower than a canonical owned Transformer-backed route
closure_requirements: freeze one canonical Transformer stack boundary above the existing Psionic substrate
implement the owned paper-faithful article Transformer path with training, replay, and receipts
replace the current fixture-shaped article model surface with a real artifact-backed Transformer model and weight lineage

article_line_refs.claim_paraphrase: the article says execution happens directly via transformer weights rather than an external tool
the article says the same transformer emits and executes the program
the article says the interpreter behavior is encoded in the model weights and could later compile programs directly into weights

blockers.title: Canonical fast decode route remains open
blockers.current_gap_summary: the article treats logarithmic-time fast decoding as the core technical unlock, but the current public closeout route is still the exact reference-linear CPU lane
blockers.current_public_truth: the repo already has fast-path runtime and research comparison surfaces, yet none is currently the canonical audited article-equivalence proof route
closure_requirements: choose one fast architecture that can become the canonical article route
integrate that fast path into the Transformer-backed article model itself
close exactness, no-fallback, and throughput floors on the selected fast path

article_line_refs.claim_paraphrase: the article says the decoding path turns attention lookups into logarithmic-time queries and enables millions of steps in a single run
the article says the key technical unlock is a log-time decoding path enabled by 2D lookup heads
the article claims exponentially faster attention lookups with logarithmic-time work per step
the article says the hull-based decoder changes long-horizon feasibility by keeping per-step cost logarithmic

blockers.title: Hungarian and hard-Sudoku benchmark parity remains open
blockers.current_gap_summary: the article makes visible Hungarian throughput claims and hard-Sudoku benchmark claims that the current public repo does not yet close on one canonical article-equivalent route
blockers.current_public_truth: the repo already has bounded Hungarian and Sudoku article reproducers plus long-horizon runtime closure, but not one canonical article demo-and-benchmark parity gate at the article's stated breadth
closure_requirements: close the Hungarian demo on the canonical article route with explicit throughput floors
close Arto Inkala and the declared hard-Sudoku benchmark suite on the canonical article route
combine those rows into one article demo-and-benchmark equivalence gate

article_line_refs.claim_paraphrase: the Hungarian demo publishes concrete token and line throughput plus total-token figures
the article states the model streams results at more than 30k tokens per second on CPU
the article claims 100 percent accuracy on the hard-Sudoku benchmarks it highlights
the article claims Arto Inkala's Sudoku is solved in under 3 minutes
the Sudoku demo publishes concrete token, line, and total-token figures

blockers.title: Single-run no-spill article posture remains open
blockers.current_gap_summary: the article frames million-step execution inside a single transformer run, while the public terminal substrate claim still permits bounded slices, persisted continuation, and spill/tape extension
blockers.current_public_truth: the repo already has named long-horizon direct execution and a bounded resumable universality substrate, but not the final no-resume single-run article gate
closure_requirements: freeze one no-resume article operator envelope over the canonical route
prove exact long-horizon execution with no checkpoint restore, spill/tape extension, hidden re-entry, or teacher forcing
keep context-length sensitivity and no-cheat boundary perturbations explicit

article_line_refs.claim_paraphrase: the article says arbitrary C-code programs can execute reliably for millions of steps in seconds
the article says the fast path enables millions of correct execution steps inside a single transformer run
the article says execution stays within the same transformer instead of pausing for an external tool

blockers.title: Clean-room weight causality and route-minimality verdict remain open
blockers.current_gap_summary: the article invites a strong reading where decisive execution behavior lives in transformer weights on a minimal route. The public repo still lacks the final clean-room causality, activation-dominance, and route-minimality verdicts for that stronger reading
blockers.current_public_truth: the repo already has direct no-tool proof receipts on committed article workloads and explicit refusal discipline, but it does not yet have the final generic interpreter-ownership gate or the final route-minimality publication verdict
closure_requirements: move direct no-tool proof and exactness onto the Transformer-backed route
close invariance, anti-memorization, and evaluation-independence evidence so correctness does not look like brittle recall
publish clean-room weight causality, KV-cache and activation-state discipline, route-minimality, and final publication verdicts

article_line_refs.claim_paraphrase: the article says the model does not call an external tool and executes directly via transformer weights
the article contrasts in-model execution as transparent and fully inside the model loop
the article says the compiled Sudoku solver runs inside the transformer itself with no verifier gap
the article says the current prototype learns an interpreter whose behavior is encoded in its weights

current_truth_boundary: the public repo has a bounded Rust-only article-closeout path, a bounded no-tool direct proof route on committed workloads, and a bounded Turing-complete substrate statement under declared `TCM.v1` semantics; it does not yet have full article-equivalent closure in the article's strongest frontend, fast-route, benchmark, single-run, or clean-room weight-ownership reading
non_implications: not a positive article-equivalence closure claim
not arbitrary C or arbitrary Wasm ingress closure
not a generic public interpreter-in-weights claim
not proof that the fast decode path is already the canonical public route
not proof that hard-Sudoku benchmark closure, no-spill single-run closure, or route-minimality closure already exist
claim_boundary: this report freezes the exact blocker set for article-equivalent closure. It is a machine-readable contract for what still has to become true. It keeps article-equivalence red by design until every blocker closes, and it must not be read as widening the current public capability surface
summary: Article-equivalence blocker matrix now freezes blocker_count=7, later_issue_count=38, matrix_contract_green=true, article_equivalence_green=false, and open_blocker_count=7.
